characters.name: Achboo
characters.description: Achboois a minor character who was only mentioned byJoshinWe're Married?. In the end credits, Josh had read the email fromYookathat Achboo was married and her father gave him the Boston Red Sox team as Drake was very upset.

characters.name: Alan Krim
characters.description: Alan Krimis the main antagonist of the two-parterDrake & Josh: Really Big Shrimp. He is the former vice president of Spin City Records who briefly managed to handle creative control of Drake Parker's music via persuading Josh Nichols, Drake's representative talent manager, into signing a music contract immediately without any consideration. He was shown to be selfish and egocentric. He wants everything to go his way. For instance, he said he liked Drake's song, but he really wanted to ask Drake to sign a contract so that he can remix the song the way he think is sounds good. Krim is the former vice president of Spin City Records and wants to help make Drake's song "Makes Me Happy" a new hit.  However, after an enraged Drake tells Josh that he needs to play dirty back, Josh sneaks into the recording studio and switches the dreadful mix back to the original. When the commercial is aired, Drake is overjoyed but Krim suddenly calls Josh to tell them that what he did was a violation of the contract and legal action could be imposed on them. However, when Drake and Josh come to Spin City Records the next day, Krim is angered to hear that fans liked Drake's original version and Spin City Records put the song available online and there were so many downloads. As a result, Drake and Josh are free of all charges and consequently Krim is fired for trying to ruin Drake's song, trying to put it on live national TV for numerous people to hear and making Drake look bad. He was shown to be selfish and egocentric. He wants everything to go his way. For instance, he said he liked Drake's song, but he really wanted to ask Drake to sign a contract so that he can remix the song the way he think is sounds good.
characters.personality_section: He was shown to be selfish and egocentric. He wants everything to go his way. For instance, he said he liked Drake's song, but he really wanted to ask Drake to sign a contract so that he can remix the song the way he think is sounds good.

characters.name: Alden Crenshaw
characters.description: Alden CrenshawisMindy Crenshaw's younger brother and appeared during the end credits ofGirl Powerwhere he yells atJoshfor taking a piece of chicken he wanted, and then giving him a chicken breast which he doesn't like. He was never seen or mentioned again. Since he only appeared in the end credits, his character was an idea that was ultimately cut and therefore he does not canonically exist in the series.

characters.name: Alice Hayfer
characters.description: Alice Hayfer(formerlyLinda Hayfer) is a recurring character onDrake & Josh. She is played by Julia Duffy. She’sDrakeandJosh's English teacher. She adores Josh, due to his hard-working personality, but hates Drake due to his consistently poor performance in school. In fact, her catchphrase is "I hate you, Drake", with Drake responding, "I know". She also always says "To the nurse!", which is her own bizarre way by telling people to leave her classroom. Mrs. Hayfer also has a teenage daughter namedKelly, who Drake briefly dated, much to her dismay. In "Honor Council", she comes into the classroom to find her car parked where Drake and Josh's seats were originally located and accuses Drake of putting the car there, but her star student,Mindy, was the real culprit for giving her a"B"and Drake proved to be innocent as well apologizes to him though she said she still hates him. In this episode, it was said that her first name was "Alice". However, in "Vicious Tiberius", her name is shown in a video as Linda Hayfer (it is possible she changed her name given said video was taped many years ago). She also appears in the television film,Merry Christmas, Drake & Joshwhile Drake is driving his car, and he hits a fire hydrant and accidentally sprays her with water while she’s walking on the sidewalk.

characters.name: Ashley Blake
characters.description: Ashley Blakeis the main antagonist in the episode,Little Diva. Portrayed as a spoiled and insolent Hollywood child actress, she is seen differently to the rest of the world, who are oblivious to her temperament. She's a parody ofDakota Fanningor Regina George in Mean Girls. She primarily plays as the main antagonist inLittle Diva. When first seen,Joshacts as a big fan of hers and his friendly hospitality is undermined by the reaction Blake presents when presented with chilled water, as opposed to room temperature water. Furthermore, this hostility towardsJosh, and laterDrake, builds a strong relationship between her andMegan, due to their strong desires to prank and tortureJoshandDrake. In addition to the water bottle incident, Blake abuses her power by being presented with a tall bodyguard, Citrus, and this is usually in place to intimidate rebellious deeds against her, including against her own brother whom she fired as her assistant for getting her bubble gum that she didn't like. This is most notable when Josh and Drake refuse to offer a soy latte when Blake busts intoJoshandDrake's house, demanding a soy latte. The unjust abuse of Citrus is later stressed towards the end ofLittle Divawhen he decides to go off with a date rather than to help an awakening Ashley.

characters.name: Audrey Parker-Nichols
characters.description:
Audrey Parker-NicholsisDrakeandMegan's biological mother,Josh's stepmother, and wife ofWalter Nichols.
She, like Megan, upsets Walter because of her preference toBruce Winchill, his rival weatherman. Because Walter is unable to discipline Drake and Josh, she usually has to punish the boys. Her name is not mentioned in the show, appears only on the Nick.com web page and when the TV captions are turned on. She, along with Walter, always believes Megan instead of Drake and Josh, even when they are telling the truth. It is unknown what her profession is or even if she works, as her job is never mentioned throughout the series. InPilot, it starts off when Audrey and Walter become engaged, and they get married but never show it. They are a very loving towards each other even though she loves Bruce Winchell due to his accurate weather reports and his hair.

characters.name: Baaahhb
characters.description: BaaahhbwasMegan's secret pet sheep who only appears inSheep Thrills. Baaahhb was pregnant and gave birth to a lamb onDrake's bed. Megansecretly ordered Baaahhb (a pun on “baaahh” and “Bob”) from thesheepstore.com without permission fromWalterandAudrey.DrakeandJoshheard Baaahhb in the garage one night and thought it was a burglar, only to end up discovering the sheep. Baaahhb was believed to be a boy until the sheep ended up giving birth to a lamb, after being assisted byDr. Jeff Glazer. Megan tells Drake and Josh that if they take care of her for a while, she will not pull any pranks on them for the next three months. However, this turns out to be a trick as when their parents find out about the sheep, Megan pretends that she has never seen Baaahhb in her life so that Drake and Josh would get grounded. Then, Baaahhb and her lamb are put in a petting zoo.

characters.name: Blaze
characters.description: Blazeis a criminal that only appeared as the main antagonist on the episodeSteered Straight. He is portrayed by Allan Louis. WhenDrakeandJoshgot caught by the police for using fake IDs (Drake as "Jefferson Steelflex" and Josh as "Alvin Yakatori") to see their favorite band "The Hailstones" (a parody ofThe Rolling Stones), their parents sent them to the Steered Straight program rather than grounding them thinking that it would teach them responsibility and what it is like to be arrested. The police officer who was taking them to the police station had his car hijacked by Blaze, shortly after he robs a convenience store.

characters.name: Bludge
characters.description: Bludgeis a character that only made one appearance inMerry Christmas, Drake & Josh. He was Josh’s cell mate when he got arrested. Bludge started off being aggressive to Josh, once Josh joined him in the jail cell. But after Josh did a magic trick on Bludge. Bludge became friends with Josh. Bludge was friendly enough that he helped Josh make Mary Alice and her foster siblings have the best Christmas, by bringing presents for Mary Alice and her foster siblings. Bludge was Josh's jail cell mate. He came down the chimney with a bag of toys just like Santa Claus. He then delivered the toys to Mary Alice and her foster siblings. Bludge started off being aggressive to Josh, once Josh joined him in the jail cell. But after Josh did a magic trick on Bludge. Bludge became friends with Josh. Bludge was friendly enough that he helped Josh make Mary Alice and her foster siblings have the best Christmas, by bringing presents for Mary Alice and her foster siblings.

characters.name: Brice Granger
characters.description: Brice Grangeris an antagonist, and an minor character.  Brice Granger appears to be very aggressive and determined as he andDeeganwent of their way to get their G-O back. He also isn't above kidnapping and murder. Brice Granger appears to be very aggressive and determined as he andDeeganwent of their way to get their G-O back. He also isn't above kidnapping and murder.

characters.name: Brittany Curran
characters.description: Brittany Curran(also known asBrittany) is an American a ctress and singer. She is known for her role as Pamela in Go Figure and as Chelsea Brimmer on The Suite Life of Zack & Cody. Curran guest starred on television shows Drake & Josh, The Suite Life of Zack & Cody, The Suite Life on Deck and Ghost Whisperer. She also had roles in the films 13 Going on 30, Akeelah and the Bee and The Haunting Hour: Don't Think About It, and was in the Disney Channel Original Movie Go Figure. Curran also appeared in an anti-drug commercial, Above the Influence. Curran's song "Quiver" leaked online in 2009. She appeared as the daughter of Ray Romano's character in the TV show, Men of a Certain Age, from 2009-11. She was also seen on a Paper Jamz commercial.

characters.name: Bruce Winchill
characters.description: Bruce Winchillis a rival weatherman who works forWalter's competition news station. He is never seen on the show but mentioned from time to time. He is described byMeganandAudreyas having good-looking hair and hated by Walter so much that at times he orders his family never to mention his rival weatherman's name in the house. Walter lost the Weather Man Award at the "Newsies" to his rival, because Winchill's forecasts were 100% accurate, far more accurate than his. Walter Nichols' accuracies were only 70%. Bruce's last name may be a pun on windchill. Although Bruce was never in the series some people might know what he looks like because of the way Megan described his hair might be what Walter looked like in the episodeThe Storm. His name was first mentioned inTheater Thug.

characters.name: Buck
characters.description: Buckis only seen inPilot, asTiffany Margolis' jock boyfriend, who mistakesJoshforDrakebecause Josh was sitting with his girlfriend, Tiffany, while Drake was in the kitchen. He challenges Josh to a fight at school and knocks him out with one punch resulting in a black eye. As he told Tiffany about Josh dating her, she told him it was Drake doing this and knock him out for good. He is known to be big and dimwitted. He was never seen again after this episode as he likely either moved away, graduated, or maybe got expelled for injuring Drake and Josh.

characters.name: Buddy & Guy
characters.description: BuddyandGuyare two wanted criminals and the main antagonists in the episodeThe Gary Grill. Buddy (who has brown hair) and Guy (who has black hair) hoped to make a profit by stealing hundreds of Gary Coleman grills fromGary Colemanhimself. To help sell the grills, they pose themselves as salesmen and presented themselves toDrakeand Josh and offered them to sell the goods. The two boys accepted the offer, but they were completely unaware that the grills were stolen and were eventually arrested by the FBI for selling stolen products, even though they tried to tell the FBI that they didn't know they were stolen and that they didn't do anything wrong after the FBI interrogated them about the Gary Coleman grills being stolen by a pair of two clean-cut Caucasian males in New Jersey. To get her brothers out of prison and clear their names, Megan tricked Buddy and Guy into confessing through a wire, and Buddy and Guy were arrested by the FBI. Then the FBI released Drake and Josh from jail after they finally discovered the truth. It is unknown what has happened to Buddy & Guy after this, but it is presumed that they are in jail for their felonious crimes never to be seen again.

characters.name: Carly
characters.description: Carlyis a recurring character who was present in a couple of Season 4 episodes only.Drakestarts dating Carly onscreen since her debut appearance, although it was seemingly temporary. Drake met her at Spin City where she worked. They apparently really liked each other, as Carly was at Drake's house. After she found out that Drake and Josh were having a bet about who could date the most girls, she became furious and wouldn't talk to Drake. Drake did many things to prove his honesty and loyalty to her, but he failed. At the end, Drake gathered many of the people he deceived and confessed what he did to them. This helped Carly to realize that Drake was honest after all, and they reconciled and resumed dating. In "The Storm", Carly appears again for her second and final time in the series. Drake spots Carly, his current girlfriend with two of his ex-girlfriends, Lucy (Gabrielle Christian) and Christine (Sara Christine Smith) thanks to Megan. Carly reveals he’s the best kisser.

characters.name: Chad Crenshaw
characters.description: Chad Crenshawis the cousin ofMindy Crenshaw. He’s a minor character, only appearing in one episode "Eric Punches Drake" out of the whole series, when Josh believes he is dating Mindy (after the two recently broke up) and becomes jealous, until Josh finds out that he's just Mindy's cousin. Chad and Mindy messed with Josh by claiming Chad wrote a novel when he was only in the 7th Grade. Oprah Winfrey was so impressed by the novel that she invited him to be on her show, later inviting him for dinner at her house. Chad also pretended to receive a call from the president of the United States by congratulating him on his success.

characters.name: Clayton
characters.description: Claytonis a minor character who attends at the same school asDrake ParkerandJosh Nichols. He is considered one of the nerdiest kids in the school by the other students. He mumbles on behalf of suffering with speech issues, and nobody can understand him. He was even called a "nerd" byCraigandEric(who are normally the ones called nerds) in "Josh Is Done", when the science teacher told them he would be partners with Drake to do the experiment. He also appears in "Eric Punches Drake", when Drake and he was looking into their locker, Drake would then spit the mouthwash into his water bottle. After that Clayton then knocks on Drake's door and says"WHY?"and shows Drake the bottle. Drake then closes and locks the door on him.

characters.name: Coach Remmers
characters.description: Coach Remmersis a minor character inDrake & Josh, who only appeared inDance Contest. He is the gym coach of Drake. Coach Remmers walked to Drake and Josh's house to let Drake know he's failing gym. Audrey walked over to introduce herself to Coach Remmers, Coach Remmers told Audrey that Drake told him his parents were lost at sea. Coach Remmers told Drake and Audrey that Drake been cutting gym, and that Drake would have to repeat the 11th Grade. Drake told him he promises to come to gym class for the rest of the semester, but Coach Remmers told Drake he would still not have enough credits to pass.

characters.name: Cookie Hancock
characters.description: Cookie HancockPortrayed byGretchen EakinsInformationGenderFemaleFull nameCookie Hancock Cookie Hancockis a character inDrake & Josh.

characters.name: Corey
characters.description: Monica(ex-girlfriend) Coreyis a minor character who only appeared as the main antagonist in "Megan's First Kiss". He wasMegan'sboyfriend untilDrakeandJoshinterfere with their relationship, since they were overprotective over Megan. Drake and Josh give him another chance going out with Megan, but only if they agree to let him have Josh's alarm clock, Drake's guitar, and both of their pants so he could date Megan again. He likes giving Japanese candy to his 2 dates: Megan and Monica. He is also a cheater, which he was found dating another girl, Monica at the same time without Megan knowing. Josh finds out about Corey cheating on Megan when Josh collects his paycheck atThe Premierefrom his boss Helen. When Drake and Josh tell Megan the truth about Corey, she got mad because she thinks they're trying to find a way to interfere with her relationship again. But sooner or later,DrakeandJoshget in contact with Corey's other girlfriend Monica and the boys end up bringing Monica toThe Premiereto have her confront Corey about him cheating. Megan then breaks up with him while Monica breaks up with Corey. He is also a very good Kung Fu fighter, since Drake and Josh got beat up at the end, because they wanted to teach Corey a lesson for cheating on Megan. Corey and Megan were dating and almost kissed, until Corey wanted to break up with Megan because he didn't want to date a girl who comes from a "freakish" family. Then Megan breaks up with Corey because he was cheating on her with Monica.

characters.name: Corey
characters.description: Monica(ex-girlfriend) Coreyis a minor character who only appeared as the main antagonist in "Megan's First Kiss". He wasMegan'sboyfriend untilDrakeandJoshinterfere with their relationship, since they were overprotective over Megan. Drake and Josh give him another chance going out with Megan, but only if they agree to let him have Josh's alarm clock, Drake's guitar, and both of their pants so he could date Megan again. He likes giving Japanese candy to his 2 dates: Megan and Monica. He is also a cheater, which he was found dating another girl, Monica at the same time without Megan knowing. Josh finds out about Corey cheating on Megan when Josh collects his paycheck atThe Premierefrom his boss Helen. When Drake and Josh tell Megan the truth about Corey, she got mad because she thinks they're trying to find a way to interfere with her relationship again. But sooner or later,DrakeandJoshget in contact with Corey's other girlfriend Monica and the boys end up bringing Monica toThe Premiereto have her confront Corey about him cheating. Megan then breaks up with him while Monica breaks up with Corey. He is also a very good Kung Fu fighter, since Drake and Josh got beat up at the end, because they wanted to teach Corey a lesson for cheating on Megan. Corey and Megan were dating and almost kissed, until Corey wanted to break up with Megan because he didn't want to date a girl who comes from a "freakish" family. Then Megan breaks up with Corey because he was cheating on her with Monica.

characters.name: Craig Ramirez
characters.description: Craig Ramirezis a recurring character in theTVshowDrake & Josh. He is best friends withEricand is mostly seen with him. Both are stereotypical "nerds" who go toBelleview High School. They're also best friends withDrakeandJosh. However, they are less nerdy thanClayton. TBA

characters.name: Crazy Steve
characters.description: #superpsychoCameo inSam and Cat(official) Steven William Upshaw(more commonly known asCrazy Steve) is a mentally disturbed employee ofThe Premiere Theatre. He made his first appearance in the season 2 episode,Movie Job. At first, he quits his job (in "Movie Job") and gives it toJoshbecause of a complaint about a loose cup holder but returns later in Seasons 3 and 4 as an employee again for unknown reasons, likely because of his handicap and state law coverage to be re-employed. He has lots of strange problems, such as extreme anger issues. It can be assumed in "Really Big Shrimp" that because of his father he had a rough childhood. He likes watching Dora the Explorer ("The Storm"), and the song "She'll Be Coming 'Round the Mountain" calms him down. He also is never scheduled to work on Mondays because those are his "bad days" as revealed in "Really Big Shrimp". Mindy once scheduled him to work on Mondays, not knowing that Mondays are his bad days, and exclaims "Crazy Steve's gone berserk!", before he comes out all goofy and says "Cocka doodle doo, the cow says moo!" InMerry Christmas, Drake & Josh, it is revealed that he has a woodchipper named Sally and gets angry when people refer to her as "it". He is shown to act normal when he talks to some characters likeAudrey. At first, he quits his job (in "Movie Job") and gives it toJoshbecause of a complaint about a loose cup holder but returns later in Seasons 3 and 4 as an employee again for unknown reasons, likely because of his handicap and state law coverage to be re-employed. He likes watching Dora the Explorer ("The Storm"), and the song "She'll Be Coming 'Round the Mountain" calms him down. InMerry Christmas, Drake & Josh, it is revealed that he has a woodchipper named Sally and gets angry when people refer to her as "it". He is shown to act normal when he talks to some characters likeAudrey. It is also implied that his problems may have gotten him into legal trouble. InThe Storm,Helenhad to invite Steve to Josh's party for Drake's concert because the last time he was not invited to an event, such as Helen's niece's bar mitzvah, he got so angry that he slashed the tires of the attendee's vehicles. Additionally, inReally Big Shrimp, when Helen announces a new Assistant Manager, she mentions that one qualification is "not having a criminal record," which made Steve yell angrily in disappointment. When he was scheduled to work on his bad day and goes berserk, Helen was planning to call the SWAT team had Josh not stepped up to

characters.name: Dave Parker
characters.description: Dave ParkerInformationGenderMaleFull nameDavid Jared ParkerFamilyDrake Parker(son)Megan Parker(daughter)Audrey Parker-Nichols(ex-wife) David "Dave" Parkeris the ex-husband ofAudrey Parker-Nicholsand the biological father ofMeganandDrake. Dave and Audrey got a divorce for an unknown reason, and it is unknown when the divorce happened. He never makes an appearance and is never mentioned.

characters.name: Deegan
characters.description: Drown and kill Drake and Josh to prevent them from informing the authorities of his plans (all failed). TheftKidnappingAttempted MurderAssault Deeganis the secondary antagonists who only appeared inDrake & Josh Go Hollywood. His name was revealed in the credits. On the plane, Deegan was calm, but rather cold and emotionless, keeping to himself, only speaking when spoken to. Once he met withBrice Granger, he was revealed to be an aggressive crook, as they went out of their way to get their G-O back. He also isn't above kidnapping and murder.

characters.name: Denise Crenshaw
characters.description: Denise CrenshawisMindy's third cousin who has only made an appearance in "Alien Invasion." She mistookDraketo be Mindy's boyfriend untilJoshtold her that he's actually her boyfriend, much to her surprise. She then helps the two turn them into aliens by putting special effects makeup on their faces, that way Drake and Josh can scareMegan. Her occupation is a special effects makeup artist for Ghost Monsters. She's portrayed by Maggie Wheeler for a guest starring role.
